concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains crucial, meaningful images, specifically the agency logos on page 1 and the full-page maps representing Figures 1-5 on pages 20-24. According to the provided image manifest, the base64 data extraction for these images failed (marked as 'valid': false with the error 'No module named PIL'). Consequently, despite tags being present in the HTML output, the image data is corrupt and these essential maps and logos will render as broken images.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Pages 20-24 contain large, meaningful maps (Figures 1-5) such as 'Figure 1. Neuse River Basin'. Page 1 contains the NCDENR and Moffatt & Nichol logos.
concerns.output_quote: The image manifest indicates that the extracted base64 data for the maps and logos is corrupt ('valid': false), meaning these image tags will render as broken.

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 2 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=2. Examples: pages 17-18: 14 cols (permit, owner, facility...); pages 18-19: 14 cols (permit, owner, facility...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 1.1x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=1.06. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (52): NEUSE BASIN MODELING - DATA COLLECTION DRAFT REPORT; April 2009; - ii -
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=52. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 5 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: http://www.agr.state.nc.us/stats/release/WU2007; http://www.ncwater.org/whichsystem.php; <http://lwf.ncdc.noaa.gov/oa/ncdc.html>; <http://www.agcensus.usda.gov/Publications/2007/index.asp>; http://www.census.gov/prod/www/abs/decennial/index.htm>
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=5. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links.

The source PDF contains several underlined titles in the 'References' section to denote document titles or specific data sets. This underline formatting was completely lost in the JSON output, which extracted the text plainly without any markdown to preserve the emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: United States Department of Agriculture: National Agricultural Statistics Service. The Census of Agriculture. 1930, 1950... [Note: 'The Census of Agriculture.' is underlined in the source PDF]
concerns.output_quote: "United States Department of Agriculture: National Agricultural Statistics Service. The Census of Agriculture. 1930, 1950, 1992, 1997, 2002, 2007. Washington: GPO, publication date varies. <http://www.agcensus.usda.gov/Publications/2007/index.asp>"

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 2 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=2, examples=['http://www.agr.state.nc.us/plantindustry/NurseryBook/search.', 'http://nc.water.usgs.gov/infodata/wateruse/data/Data_Tables_']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Numbered items as headings: 4 heading(s) look like numbered list items
concerns.analysis: heading_list_count=4. Headings starting with '1.', '2.' etc. suggest numbered list items were misclassified as headings during extraction.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=1. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

concerns.description: Source PDF contains 3 instance(s) of blue-styled text without hyperlink annotations. These appear to be intended as hyperlinks but were not annotated in the PDF (likely lost during Word-to-PDF export). This is a source document limitation, not a pipeline defect. Examples: page 11: "http://www.agr.state.nc.us/plantindustry/"; page 11: "NurseryBook/search.asp"; page 11: "http://nc.water.usgs.gov/infodata/wateruse/data/Da"
concerns.analysis: orphaned_blue_text_count=3. Blue text without link annotations detected via PyMuPDF color extraction.
